penalties.description: Task 5 requires intracorporeal suturing of Penrose drain, but video shows only extracorporeal Roeder's knot formation

confidence_rationale: Video clearly shows extracorporeal Roeder's knot formation rather than the required intracorporeal suturing task. Text overlay confirms 'Roeder's knot' technique. No Penrose drain, needle drivers, or intracorporeal suturing visible.
cannot_determine: Actual suture placement accuracy - task was not performed
Drain gap assessment - no drain present
Individual throw assessments - wrong knot type performed
technique_summary: Video demonstrates extracorporeal Roeder's knot technique rather than FLS Task 5 intracorporeal suturing. While knot formation appears technically sound for Roeder's technique, this does not fulfill Task 5 requirements.
strengths: Good bilateral hand coordination during manipulation
improvement_suggestions: Review FLS Task 5 requirements - must perform intracorporeal suturing of Penrose drain
Practice needle loading and tissue penetration with needle drivers
Focus on surgeon's knot (double throw) followed by single throws, not Roeder's knot
